ucs.title: [LLM] FIN5 dormant VPN/Citrix valid-account reuse followed by 'net' recon burst
ucs.description: Hunts FIN5's signature initial-access chain: returning months later through a stolen/brute-forced legitimate VPN, Citrix, or Outlook Web App account (no exploit, no malware in delivery), then immediately enumerating domain assets with native net.exe / nltest commands to locate POS environments in restaurant, gaming, and hotel networks.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstAuth max(_time) as lastAuth from datamodel=Authentication where Authentication.action=success Authentication.app IN ("vpn","citrix","owa","radius","globalprotect","anyconnect") by Authentication.user Authentication.src Authentication.dest 
| `drop_dm_object_name(Authentication)` 
| eval daysSinceLastSeen=round((firstAuth - relative_time(now(),"-180d"))/86400,1) 
| where firstAuth > relative_time(now(),"-7d@d") 
| join type=inner user [
    | tstats summariesonly=true values(Processes.process) as recon_cmds dc(Processes.dest) as recon_hostcount count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("net.exe","net1.exe","nltest.exe","systeminfo.exe","quser.exe","tasklist.exe") (Processes.process="*view*" OR Processes.process="*group*\"domain admins\"*" OR Processes.process="*localgroup*" OR Processes.process="*/domain_trusts*" OR Processes.process="*session*" OR Processes.process="*config workstation*") by Processes.user Processes.dest _time span=1h 
    | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` 
    | rename user as user 
    | where recon_hostcount>=3 ] 
| eval risk_score = case(recon_hostcount>=10,90, recon_hostcount>=5,70, true(),50) 
| table _time user src dest recon_cmds recon_hostcount risk_score
ucs.kql:
let lookback = 7d;
let dormancy = 60d;
let remoteAuths = IdentityLogonEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
  | where ActionType == "LogonSuccess"
  | where LogonType in ("Network","RemoteInteractive") or Protocol in~ ("Vpn","Radius","Citrix")
  | summarize firstSeenInWindow=min(Timestamp), srcIPs=make_set(IPAddress,10) by AccountName, AccountDomain;
let historicalAuths = IdentityLogonEvents
  | where Timestamp between (ago(dormancy) .. ago(lookback))
  | summarize lastHistoricalAuth=max(Timestamp) by AccountName;
let returningAccts = remoteAuths
  | join kind=leftouter historicalAuths on AccountName
  | where isnull(lastHistoricalAuth) or datetime_diff('day', firstSeenInWindow, lastHistoricalAuth) > 30;
DeviceProcessEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
  | where FileName in~ ("net.exe","net1.exe","nltest.exe","quser.exe","tasklist.exe","systeminfo.exe")
  | where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("view /domain","group \"domain admins\"","localgroup administrators","/domain_trusts","session","config workstation","/dclist")
  | extend AccountName = tolower(AccountName)
  | join kind=inner (returningAccts | extend AccountName = tolower(AccountName)) on AccountName
  | summarize reconCount=count(), reconHosts=dcount(DeviceId), cmds=make_set(ProcessCommandLine,15), srcIPs=any(srcIPs) by AccountName, bin(Timestamp,1h)
  | where reconCount >= 5 or reconHosts >= 3
  | order by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: FIN5 is uniquely characterized by patient, malware-free initial access — they steal or brute legitimate remote-access credentials and return after long gaps (Mandiant has documented FIN5 dwell times of 6+ months and re-entry through old credentials). Pairing 'returning user on remote service' with an immediate net.exe/nltest recon burst across multiple hosts is FIN5's documented first-hour playboo

ucs.title: [LLM] FIN5 RawPOS-style track-data staging with log wipe (T1074.001 + T1070.004)
ucs.description: Detects FIN5's terminal-stage tradecraft on POS servers: their RawPOS scraper dumping cardholder Track 1/Track 2 data into local staging files in C:\Windows or %TEMP%, followed by event-log clearing and file deletion via wevtutil/del to cover tracks before FLIPSIDE proxy exfiltration.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count values(Filesystem.file_path) as staged_files values(Filesystem.file_name) as staged_names from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.action=created (Filesystem.file_path="*\\Windows\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Temp\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\PerfLogs\\*") (Filesystem.file_name="*.dat" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.tmp" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.log" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.txt" OR Filesystem.file_name="dr*.dll" OR Filesystem.file_name="duebrew*" OR Filesystem.file_name="FIESTA*") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.user _time span=15m 
| `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` 
| join type=inner dest [
    | tstats summariesonly=true values(Processes.process) as wipe_cmds count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("wevtutil.exe","cmd.exe","powershell.exe","fsutil.exe")) (Processes.process="*wevtutil* cl *" OR Processes.process="*Clear-EventLog*" OR Processes.process="*del /f /q*" OR Processes.process="*usn deletejournal*" OR Processes.process="*cipher /w*") by Processes.dest _time span=1h 
    | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` ] 
| eval risk_score = if(match(staged_names,"(?i)duebrew|fiesta|driverframe"),95,70) 
| table _time dest user staged_files staged_names wipe_cmds risk_score
ucs.kql:
let stage = DeviceFileEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
  | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileModified")
  | where FolderPath matches regex @"(?i)\\(Windows|Temp|ProgramData|PerfLogs|Users\\Public)\\"
  | where FileName matches regex @"(?i)\.(dat|tmp|log|txt|dll)$"
     or FileName matches regex @"(?i)(duebrew|driverframe|fiesta|rawpos|getmypass)"
  | extend stagePath = FolderPath, stageFile = FileName, stageProc = InitiatingProcessFileName
  | project DeviceId, DeviceName, stageTime=Timestamp, stagePath, stageFile, stageProc, InitiatingProcessAccountName;
let wipe = DeviceProcessEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
  | where (FileName =~ "wevtutil.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has " cl ")
     or (FileName in~ ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe") and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("Clear-EventLog","del /f /q","del /q /f","Remove-Item -Force"))
     or (FileName =~ "fsutil.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "usn deletejournal")
     or (FileName =~ "cipher.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "/w")
  | project DeviceId, wipeTime=Timestamp, wipeCmd=ProcessCommandLine, wipeAcct=AccountName;
stage
  | join kind=inner wipe on DeviceId
  | where wipeTime between (stageTime .. stageTime + 6h)
  | summarize stagedFiles=make_set(strcat(stagePath,stageFile),25), wipeCmds=make_set(wipeCmd,10), firstStage=min(stageTime), lastWipe=max(wipeTime)
      by DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName
  | extend trackDataLikely = iff(array_length(stagedFiles) >= 3, true, false)
  | join kind=leftouter (
      DeviceNetworkEvents
      | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
      | where RemotePort in (443, 8443, 53) and InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","outlook.exe","teams.exe")
      | summarize exfilBytes=sum(toint(coalesce(tostring(parse_json(AdditionalFields).BytesSent),"0"))), remoteIPs=make_set(RemoteIP,10) by DeviceName
  ) on DeviceName
  | order by lastWipe desc
ucs.rationale: This is FIN5's signature end-game in POS environments documented by Mandiant/FireEye: RawPOS (and its loader components like DUEBREW/DRIVERFRAMEWORKS, plus the GET2 downloader) writes scraped magnetic-stripe data to staging files in system folders, then operators run wevtutil cl and del /f to destroy evidence before exfil through the FLIPSIDE TLS proxy. The combination of (a) non-browser file crea
